name: Ulundhu Kali with Buttermilk
description: This morning dish leans on traditional South Indian technique but shifts the grain balance toward whole grains and lentils. Ulundhu Kali with Buttermilk brings a distinct structure, seasoning pattern, and grain-legume balance to the table.
cooking_steps: Bring 200 ml water with salt, cumin, and ginger to a boil over medium heat.
Whisk in the slurry steadily and lower the heat. Cook for 6 to 8 minutes, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and leaves the sides of the pan.
Switch to low heat and beat in the warm buttermilk a little at a time until smooth and glossy.
Cook 1 final minute, then serve soft with a spoon rather than letting it set fully firm.
meal_prep_notes: Kali thickens as it cools. Refrigerate up to 2 days and reheat over low heat with extra hot water or buttermilk to loosen. For make-ahead service, stop cooking when slightly looser than desired; it firms on standing.
